title: Portugal vs DR Congo Preview, Starting XI & Prediction - 2026 World Cup
original_text:
Portugal begins their Group K campaign against the Democratic Republic of Congo (DR Congo), which appears to be a promising start for the Europeans. With Cristiano Ronaldo still leading the team at the age of 41, Portugal is expected to dominate and secure three points early in the tournament. DR Congo is returning to the World Cup after a lengthy absence and will view this match as an opportunity to make a statement against one of the favorites to win.

Portugal have looked sharp in recent friendlies and carry plenty of attacking quality across the pitch. DR Congo have shown resilience in African football and will rely on their organized defense and counter-attacking threat.

The mood in the Portuguese camp is confident, while DR Congo are motivated to compete despite being underdogs.

This Portugal vs DR Congo matchup pits Portugal’s technical superiority and experience against DR Congo’s physicality and defensive discipline. Portugal will want to control possession and break down the defense quickly, while DR Congo will look to frustrate and hit on the break. Expect Portugal to be the more attacking side from the first whistle.

They play with a fluid attacking style built around quick passing and width. Portugal dominated their qualification group and have looked comfortable in warm-ups. Their main goal here is to start strong, control the game in midfield, and use their star attackers to create early chances.

DR Congo are compact and dangerous on the counter, often relying on physical presence and quick transitions. They have shown organization in recent games. They plan to stay disciplined at the back and look for opportunities to hit Portugal on the break with players like Yoane Wissa.

Portugal have a largely fit squad. Cristiano Ronaldo is expected to start and lead the attack. Roberto Martínez has good depth across the squad, with no major reported injuries affecting the starting lineup.

DR Congo have some experienced players available but may be without a couple of key names due to minor issues. The squad is motivated for their World Cup return, with Chancel Mbemba likely to anchor the defense.

Diogo Costa; João Cancelo, Rúben Dias, Gonçalo Inácio, Nuno Mendes; Bernardo Silva, João Neves, Bruno Fernandes; Rafael Leão, Cristiano Ronaldo, Pedro Neto.

Lionel Mpasi; Aaron Wan-Bissaka, Chancel Mbemba, Axel Tuanzebe, Arthur Masuaku; Samuel Moutoussamy, Edo Kayembe; Gaël Kakuta, Théo Bongonda, Meschak Elia; Yoane Wissa.

The sides have limited recent meetings. Portugal generally hold the upper hand in encounters with African teams at this level. DR Congo will be looking to make history in their World Cup appearance.

Portugal are clear favorites and should control most of the game with their superior quality and attacking depth. DR Congo will defend deep and look for counters, but Portugal’s creativity is likely to break them down.

Portugal should get the job done comfortably after a strong performance, with Ronaldo or one of the attacking midfielders proving decisive.

In the United States, you can watch the Portugal vs DR Congo FIFA World Cup 2026 Group K match live at 1:00 PM ET / 10:00 AM PT on multiple platforms.
